senses.definition: the act or process of selling or advertising a product or service, usually including market research

examples.x: Successful marketing requires understanding customer needs.
Her job involves designing marketing strategies for new products.
Social media has become a key tool in modern marketing.

senses.definition: the promotion of a particular product or company by means of advertising and distinctive design
the activity of drawing public attention to a service or product in order to help it sell more

examples.x: The store is running a promotion this weekend, offering discounts on selected items.
He was thrilled to learn that he had been offered a promotion to manager of his department.
She took advantage of the promotion to upgrade her airline ticket to first class at a discounted rate.
The company invested heavily in online advertising as part of its promotion strategy for the new product launch.
The promotion campaign featured catchy slogans and eye-catching visuals to attract potential customers.

senses.definition: a specific group of people that a product, service, or message is aimed at or intended for

examples.x: The advertising campaign was designed with a young adult target audience in mind.
The target audience for this product includes tech-savvy professionals and entrepreneurs.
To reach the right consumers, companies must identify their target audience before launching a product.
The target audience of the book seems to be teenagers interested in fantasy novels.
When creating content, it’s important to consider the target audience's interests and preferences.

senses.definition: the combination of visual and emotional elements that distinguish a brand and shape its perception among consumers

examples.x: Their brand identity is all about being modern, sustainable, and innovative.
Every aspect of the packaging is designed to reflect the brand identity, from the colors to the font.
The new advertising campaign focuses on strengthening the brand identity and making it more relatable to younger customers.
A strong brand identity can help a small business stand out in a crowded market.

senses.definition: a company that helps other businesses to create and promote advertisements for their products or services

examples.x: The advertising agency came up with a creative strategy to target younger consumers.
He joined an advertising agency to gain experience in media planning and strategy.
She works at an advertising agency, where she develops digital campaigns for different brands.
They decided to collaborate with an advertising agency to improve their brand's visibility.

senses.definition: a specific instruction or prompt given to the audience, encouraging them to take a desired action, such as making a purchase, signing up for a newsletter, contacting a business, or engaging in some form of interaction or response

examples.x: The advertisement included a CTA that encouraged people to "Join the Club" for discounts.
The flyer’s CTA said, "Call Now to Book Your Appointment," making it easy to take the next step.
The social media post had a simple CTA, telling followers to "Click Here for More Information."
At the end of the blog post, there was a CTA inviting readers to "Download the Free Guide."

senses.definition: an advertisement broadcast on TV or radio

examples.x: The company's new product was promoted through a series of television commercials.
She starred in a commercial for a popular brand of shampoo.
The radio commercial for the upcoming concert attracted a large audience.

senses.definition: a piece of advertisement in a newspaper or magazine, designed to seem like an objective article and not an advertisement

examples.x: The magazine published an advertorial about the latest skincare products, blending informative content with promotional messaging.
Companies use advertorials to provide readers with engaging stories that subtly promote their products or services.
The advertorial on healthy eating included tips and recipes, while also promoting a new line of organic food products.

senses.definition: a form of advertising that is typically text-based and organized into specific categories, such as job listings, real estate, vehicles, and personal services

examples.x: He found his apartment through a classified advertising website.
The classified advertising section in the magazine had listings for various freelance opportunities.
When looking for a used car, I checked the classified advertising section of the newspaper.

senses.definition: the promotion of products, services, or brands using digital channels and technologies to reach and engage with specific audiences online

examples.x: Many companies use digital advertising to reach customers through social media platforms like Facebook and Instagram.
Digital advertising has become an essential part of modern marketing strategies.
Businesses often target specific age groups with digital advertising to make sure the right people see their ads.
Digital advertising allows you to track how many people have clicked on your ad or visited your website.
With digital advertising, companies can change their ads in real-time based on customer reactions and data.

senses.definition: a statement, especially by someone famous, as a form of advertisement claiming they are approved of a product

examples.x: The celebrity’s endorsement of the new brand boosted its sales significantly.
She gave her endorsement to the charity during the fundraising campaign.
The product has been marketed with several endorsements from athletes.
The company's success is partly due to the endorsement of top influencers.
The car manufacturer used a famous actor’s endorsement in their latest commercial.

senses.definition: a brief advertisement or announcement that is published in a newspaper, magazine, etc.

examples.x: She placed a notice in the local newspaper to sell her old furniture.
The job notice in the classifieds caught his attention, and he applied immediately.
The community center posted a notice about the upcoming charity event.
The real estate agent placed a notice in the realty magazine to showcase the new property.
The company issued a public notice regarding the change in office hours.

senses.definition: a printed promotional material, such as a leaflet or flyer, that is included within a publication to provide additional information or advertising content
the act of writing text for advertising or other forms of marketing communications to persuade or influence people to take some action
a symbol or design used to represent a company or organization

examples.x: The company's new logo features a bold design with vibrant colors that stand out.
The logo on the product packaging helped me recognize the brand instantly.
Every time I see that logo, I think of quality and reliability.
The logo for the charity was simple but very memorable.
They printed the logo on all their marketing materials to make sure people noticed it.

senses.definition: a short memorable phrase that is used in advertising to draw people's attention toward something

examples.x: The company's slogan "Just Do It" became synonymous with their brand, inspiring millions of athletes worldwide.
The politician's slogan focused on hope and change, aiming to rally voters for the upcoming election.
The marketing team spent weeks brainstorming the perfect slogan to capture the essence of their new product.
Every time the commercial aired, the catchy slogan stuck in viewers' minds, prompting them to visit the website.
The environmental group's slogan "Save the Earth, One Step at a Time" resonated deeply with the public during their campaign.

senses.definition: the use of printed media, such as newspapers, magazines, brochures, flyers, or direct mail, to communicate and promote products, services, or messages to a targeted audience
a message or advertisement that promotes public welfare or safety, typically created by government agencies or nonprofit organizations

examples.x: The local radio station aired a public service announcement reminding people to stay home during the storm.
There was a public service announcement on TV warning about the dangers of texting while driving.
A public service announcement appeared in the newspaper, urging people to recycle more.
The government released a public service announcement about the importance of getting vaccinated.

senses.definition: a short video advertisement that promotes a product, service, or brand on television
a brief audio advertisement that promotes a product, service, or brand on radio stations

a short video or audio clip that is placed before or after a commercial break, typically used to introduce or transition into a commercial advertisement, provide branding, or deliver a brief message to viewers or listeners
advertisements or promotional messages displayed in public spaces, such as billboards and digital screens, targeting audiences outside of their homes or workplaces

examples.x: The city council put up outdoor media to inform the public about the new recycling program.
I saw an outdoor media ad for a concert while walking to the train station.
Companies often use outdoor media to promote their brand during peak shopping seasons.
The new movie was advertised on outdoor media, with giant billboards all over the city.

senses.definition: a short catchy tune, often used in advertising

examples.x: The jingle for that soda brand is stuck in my head all day.
The commercial ended with a cheerful jingle promoting the new car model.
They hired a popular musician to create a catchy jingle for their campaign.
The jingle was so memorable that people started humming it everywhere.
She wrote a fun jingle that helped the brand’s sales soar.

senses.definition: a type of advertising or informational material that is inserted into a package or product

examples.x: The magazine came with an outsert offering a discount on the next issue.
The outsert on the newspaper included a coupon for a local restaurant.
I noticed the outsert attached to the magazine, advertising the latest movie release.
The outsert in the weekend paper was filled with information about a charity fundraiser.

senses.definition: the gradual and increasing infiltration of advertisements into various aspects of daily life, often extending beyond traditional advertising spaces
the allocation of advertising budgets across different media channels, determining the relative proportion of investment to reach the target audience effectively

examples.x: The company increased its media weight this year to ensure its new product gets maximum exposure.
With a heavy media weight during the holiday season, the brand's sales soared.
The campaign had a large media weight across both TV and social media platforms.
Advertisers carefully manage media weight to avoid overwhelming their audience with too many ads.
The small business could not compete with the larger companies that have a much higher media weight.

senses.definition: the amplification of an advertising message or campaign's impact through multiple channels or platforms, maximizing the reach, engagement, and overall effectiveness of the advertising efforts
the process of identifying the most effective combination of media channels to reach a target audience with a message, product, or service

examples.x: The team spent weeks on media planning to ensure the campaign reached as many people as possible.
Successful media planning helps businesses choose the right platforms for their advertisements.
Without proper media planning, the advertisement might not reach the target audience.
Effective media planning helped the brand achieve a significant increase in visibility.

senses.definition: a marketing strategy in which promotional materials, such as letters, postcards, brochures, or catalogs, are sent directly to potential customers or targeted recipients through the postal service

examples.x: The company sent out direct mail to promote their new product line to local customers.
Nonprofit organizations often use direct mail to ask for donations during the holiday season.
The marketing team decided to use direct mail to target customers who do not use social media.
Businesses sometimes include coupons in direct mail to attract more customers.

senses.definition: a short promotional description of a book, motion picture, etc. published on the cover of a book or in an advertisement

examples.x: The blurb on the back cover of the novel provides a brief summary of the plot to entice readers.
The movie's promotional materials featured a glowing blurb from a renowned film critic praising the performances and storytelling.
Authors often collaborate with publishers to craft compelling blurbs that capture the essence of their books and appeal to potential readers.
The blurb on the advertisement for the new smartphone highlighted its sleek design, advanced features, and competitive price.
When browsing books online, readers often rely on blurbs to help them decide whether a particular title is worth exploring further.

senses.definition: a small advertisement published in a newspaper or website, expressing requirements of a person or company

examples.x: The company placed a want ad in the local newspaper seeking applicants for an administrative assistant position.
The want ad for the apartment rental specified the desired amenities, location, and rental price range.
The community center posted a want ad for volunteers to help with upcoming events and programs.

senses.definition: an ad or notice in a publication, categorically arranged, offering goods, services, jobs, or information

examples.x: Job seekers scanned the classifieds section to find employment opportunities matching their skills and experience.

senses.definition: specialized or focused on a specific market or audience
a form of multi-level marketing where profit is derived primarily from recruiting others into the scheme, rather than from legitimate product sales
a novel device or strategy designed to attract attention or increase appeal, often considered superficial or short-lived

examples.x: The cereal company used a collectible toy as a gimmick to attract more young customers to buy their product.
The restaurant's new dish came with a unique presentation gimmick, where the food was served on a miniature Ferris wheel.
Many shoppers were drawn to the store by the promotional gimmick of a free gift with every purchase.
The politician's campaign relied heavily on a catchy slogan as a gimmick to grab voters' attention.
The tech startup introduced a gimmick feature in their app that allowed users to change the background to whimsical themes, generating buzz and downloads.

senses.definition: a business entity, organization, or company engaged in commercial, industrial, or professional activities

examples.x: The multinational concern operates in various countries, specializing in the production and distribution of consumer electronics.
As a leading concern in the automotive industry, the company constantly innovates to stay ahead in the competitive market.
The family-owned concern has been a cornerstone of the local community for decades, contributing to both economic and social development.
The technology concern recently launched a groundbreaking product, garnering attention and admiration within the industry.
Employees appreciate the employee-centric policies implemented by the human resources department of the concern, fostering a positive work environment.

senses.definition: a brief, persuasive speech used to spark interest in an idea, product, or project, typically lasting no more than 20-30 seconds
the marketing strategies to promote a product a brand, often relying on creativity and surprise to attract attention
a plan to differentiate a product or brand in the minds of consumers, often by emphasizing unique qualities or things that set it apart from competitors

examples.x: The luxury car brand developed a positioning strategy that emphasized its vehicles' superior performance and exclusivity, appealing to high-income customers who value prestige.
A health food company implemented a positioning strategy by marketing their products as organic, non-GMO, and sustainably sourced, targeting environmentally conscious consumers.
The tech startup's positioning strategy focused on its innovative and user-friendly interface, differentiating itself from competitors by highlighting ease of use and cutting-edge technology.
A budget airline employed a positioning strategy that emphasized low fares and no-frills service, attracting price-sensitive travelers looking for affordable flight options.
The skincare brand's positioning strategy revolved around its use of natural and hypoallergenic ingredients, positioning itself as the go-to choice for individuals with sensitive skin.
